title: NIS2 Business Continuity and ICT Continuity Evidence
exclusions: Does not perform business-impact analysis for every subsidiary, does not certify supplier continuity arrangements on its own, and does not replace board-approved crisis communications plans or legal filings to regulators.
evidence: Weekly `UC-22.2.24` report in PDF, underlying lookup revision hash, and correlated ServiceNow incident IDs stored under `index=audit_evidence` with reviewer attestation fields.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(c) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) (Business continuity and crisis management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.24: NIS2 Business Continuity and ICT Continuity Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.24' running on index=itsi_summary, nis2_bcp_test_register.csv, index=itsm (major incidents), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: After intentionally letting a test service miss its annual tabletop date and simulating a 45-minute Sev-1 in ITSI, the report lists that service with `months_since>12` and `rto_breach=1` when `rto_minutes` is set to 30.
controlTest.negativeScenario: When the register row is current within 12 months and ITSI shows no critical episodes beyond a 2-minute synthetic ping glitch suppressed by the duration filter, the weekly run returns no qualifying rows and the empty output is archived as the passing control sample.

dataSources: `index=itsi` `sourcetype=itsi:service_health` fields such as service_name, service_health_score, alert_severity; `index=itsi_summary` KPI rollups with alert_duration and severity for outage math; `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:incident` records (number, category, u_priority, opened_at, resolved_at) for crisis tickets tagged ICT continuity; CSV `nis2_bcp_test_register.csv` columns service_name, last_tabletop_date, rto_minutes. Apps: Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
app: Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_bcp_test_register.csv
| eval test_date_epoch=strptime(last_tabletop_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since=round((now()-test_date_epoch)/2592000,1)
| where months_since > 12
| join service_name [
    search index=itsi_summary earliest=-90d
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High")
    | stats sum(alert_duration) as downtime_seconds by service_name
]
| eval rto_seconds=rto_minutes*60
| eval rto_breach=if(downtime_seconds > rto_seconds, 1, 0)
| table service_name, months_since, downtime_seconds, rto_seconds, rto_breach
description: Combines a register of when each in-scope service last proved its continuity plan (tabletop or drill) with measured outage seconds from ITSI summaries so you can see both testing cadence slip and real recoveries that exceeded the declared recovery-time objective.
value: Executives answering NIS2 questions need more than a policy binder—they need dated proof that exercises happened and that major outages stayed inside agreed time boxes; this automation stitches telemetry and ticket metadata into one reviewer-friendly table instead of three separate spreadsheets.
implementation: (1) Maintain `nis2_bcp_test_register.csv` under `lookups/` with one row per critical service and numeric `rto_minutes`. (2) Map `service_name` values to ITSI service IDs so `join` keys stay stable. (3) Install ITSI (1841); verify `index=itsi_summary` receives alert_duration populated by glass tables or deep dives. (4) Configure ServiceNow add-on (1928) `inputs.conf` modular input polling `/api/now/table/incident` into `index=itsm` as `sourcetype=snow:incident`. (5) Schedule `UC-22.2.24` weekly; email compliance when `months_since>12` or `rto_breach=1`.
visualization: Table (overdue tests and RTO breaches), Timeline (tests and incidents), Single value (services breaching RTO). Key fields: `downtime_seconds`, `months_since`, `rto_breach`, `rto_seconds`, `service_name`, `test_date_epoch`.
knownFalsePositives:
**ServiceNow backlog during Sev-1** — Distinguish: incidents created late, priority field flipped after triage; ITSI already counted downtime. Suppress: require `opened_at` within **30 minutes** of ITSI start via correlated join, else flag as data-quality not breach.

**Planned DR drills** — Distinguish: change record `CHG*` linked, ITSI maintenance suppresses alerts. Suppress: `lookup dr_drill_windows.csv service_name OUTPUT in_drill` and add `where in_drill!=1` as time-bound exception.

**Duplicate ITSI episodes** — Distinguish: same outage split across two services due to bad entity merge; `downtime_seconds` double counts. Suppress: dedupe with `stats max(downtime_seconds) by service_name, incident_id` AFTER joining ServiceNow `number` field.

**Stale register row** — Distinguish: service decommissioned but CSV not retired shows “overdue tabletop.” Suppress: maintain `nis2_bcp_test_register.csv` status column with `where status!="retired"` filter; track removals in your exception register.

**Synthetic monitoring blips** — Distinguish: sub-minute TCP checks flip ITSI severity without user impact. Suppress: require `alert_duration` > **300 seconds** in the subsearch with `where alert_duration>300` before summing.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, BCP/DR plan and exercise tracking)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, incident response metrics)
Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) (Splunkbase 4481, service health and KPI monitoring)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• This UC implements NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) — business continuity, such as backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management. It provides evidence that the entity's Business Continuity Plan (BCP) and ICT Continuity Plan are maintained, tested, and effective. Specifically, it monitors three elements: (1) BIA (Business Impact Analysis) currency — are BIA documents reviewed and current? (2) DR exercise completion — have disaster recovery exercises been conducted on schedule? (3) ICT service recovery capability — are recovery time objectives (RTO) being met during exercises?
• **Delineation from UC-22.2.17 and UC-22.2.53**: UC-22.2.17 provides real-time backup job monitoring (per-job, per-host, hourly). UC-22.2.53 is a composite evidence roll-up covering backup, environmental, and physical security. This UC (22.2.24) focuses on the strategic continuity programme: BIA currency, DR exercise scheduling, and RTO validation. It is the governance layer on top of the operational UCs.
• **Data sources**:
  - `nis2_bcp_register.csv` — master register of BCP/DR plans and exercises. Columns: `plan_id` (e.g., "BCP-HQ-2026"), `plan_type` (bcp, dr, ict_continuity, crisis_management), `scope` (e.g., "Head Office", "Cloud infrastructure", "OT network"), `bia_review_date` (date of last BIA review), `bia_next_review` (date of next required BIA review), `exercise_cadence_months` (how often DR exercises are required — typically 6 or 12), `last_exercise_date` (date of most recent exercise), `exercise_result` (pass, partial, fail), `rto_target_hours` (the RTO from the BIA), `rto_achieved_hours` (actual recovery time in the last exercise), `owner` (plan owner).
  - `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request` — ServiceNow change requests tagged as DR exercises. Used to verify that exercises are formally managed through the change process.
  - `index=itsi sourcetype=itsi:kpi_summary` (optional) — ITSI KPI data for service availability. Used to measure actual service health during DR exercises and real incidents.
• **ITSI integration**: if ITSI is deployed, link BCP plans to ITSI services. During a DR exercise, ITSI measures the actual recovery time by tracking when the service health score drops to 0 (outage) and when it returns to the green threshold (recovered). This provides an objective `rto_achieved_hours` measurement independent of manual recording.
• **ISO 22301 alignment**: this UC aligns with ISO 22301:2019 (Business continuity management systems) clauses 8.3 (BIA), 8.5 (exercising and testing), and 9.1 (monitoring and measurement). NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) references these concepts directly.
• **RBAC**: role `nis2_bcp_analyst` with `srchIndexesAllowed = itsm, itsi_summary, audit_evidence`. BCP/DR plans reveal the organisation's recovery priorities and weaknesses — restrict access.
• **Index retention**: `index=audit_evidence` ≥ 7 years.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**BCP register CSV:**
Place `nis2_bcp_register.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-bcp/lookups/`. Define in `transforms.conf`:
```ini
[nis2_bcp_register]
filename = nis2_bcp_register.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
```
The CSV is maintained by the Head of Business Continuity or the CISO. Populate with all BCP, DR, ICT continuity, and crisis management plans. Include both corporate and site-specific plans.

Validate: `| inputlookup nis2_bcp_register.csv | stats count dc(plan_type) as types values(plan_type) as type_list dc(owner) as owners`.

**ServiceNow change requests for DR exercises:**
Configure a filter for DR exercises: `category=DR_Exercise OR short_description="*disaster recovery*"`. The UC joins exercise change requests with the BCP register to verify that exercises are formally tracked.

Verify: `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request category=DR_Exercise earliest=-1y | stats count dc(cmdb_ci) as systems values(state) as states`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.24 NIS2 Business Continuity Evidence`.

Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

• **Three detection conditions from the CSV**:
  - **BIA review overdue**: `now() > strptime(bia_next_review, "%Y-%m-%d")` identifies plans whose BIA hasn't been reviewed by the scheduled date. A stale BIA means recovery priorities may not reflect current business needs.
  - **DR exercise overdue**: `(now() - strptime(last_exercise_date, "%Y-%m-%d")) / 86400 > exercise_cadence_months * 30` identifies plans that haven't been exercised within their required cadence. An untested DR plan may not work when needed.
  - **RTO breach in last exercise**: `rto_achieved_hours > rto_target_hours` identifies plans where the last exercise failed to meet the recovery time objective. This means the DR capability is insufficient.

• **`eval days_since_exercise=round((now() - strptime(last_exercise_date, "%Y-%m-%d")) / 86400, 0)`**: provides a continuous metric of exercise staleness. Useful for trend analysis.

• **`eval rto_margin=round(rto_target_hours - rto_achieved_hours, 1)`**: negative margin means the exercise exceeded the RTO — a continuity risk.

Schedule: cron `0 8 1 * *` (monthly, first day at 08:00). BCP activities operate on monthly/quarterly cycles. Trigger: `Number of results > 0`.

Alert actions: (1) For overdue BIA reviews: email to the plan owner and Head of Business Continuity. (2) For overdue exercises: email to the CISO — regulatory risk. (3) For RTO breaches: email to the DR coordinator with the plan details. (4) Write to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:bcp:evidence`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **BIA overdue**: set `bia_next_review` to last month for a test plan. Run the search. Confirm the plan appears as overdue.

(b) **Exercise overdue**: set `last_exercise_date` to 13 months ago and `exercise_cadence_months=12`. Run the search. Confirm the plan appears as exercise-overdue.

(c) **RTO breach**: set `rto_target_hours=4` and `rto_achieved_hours=6`. Run the search. Confirm `rto_margin=-2`.

(d) **Clean plan**: set all dates to current, `rto_achieved_hours < rto_target_hours`. Run the search. Confirm no alert.

(e) **ITSI integration validation (if applicable)**: during a scheduled DR exercise, monitor the ITSI service health score. Record the actual outage duration. Compare with the manually recorded `rto_achieved_hours` in the CSV. They should agree within 15 minutes.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Business Continuity Readiness`):
• **Row 1 — Continuity posture**: Single-value `Plans registered`. Single-value `BIA reviews overdue` (red ≥ 1). Single-value `Exercises overdue` (red ≥ 1). Single-value `RTO breaches` (red ≥ 1).
• **Row 2 — Plan register table**: all plans with `plan_id`, `plan_type`, `scope`, `bia_next_review`, `last_exercise_date`, `rto_target_hours`, `rto_achieved_hours`, `rto_margin`, `owner`. Colour-code rows: green=all current, amber=due soon, red=overdue or RTO breach.
• **Row 3 — Exercise timeline**: `| inputlookup nis2_bcp_register.csv | eval exercise_epoch=strptime(last_exercise_date,"%Y-%m-%d") | sort exercise_epoch | table plan_id scope last_exercise_date exercise_result`. Gantt-style or timeline showing when exercises occurred.
• **Row 4 — RTO performance**: bar chart of `rto_target_hours` vs `rto_achieved_hours` by plan_id. Target line at the RTO boundary.

Runbook (owner: Head of Business Continuity / CISO):
1. Monthly: review the alert. For overdue BIA reviews: schedule a BIA review workshop with business stakeholders. Update impact categories, RTOs, and dependencies.
2. Quarterly: review exercise schedule. Ensure all plans due for exercise in the next quarter are scheduled with ServiceNow change tickets.
3. After each exercise: update the CSV with `last_exercise_date`, `exercise_result`, `rto_achieved_hours`. If the exercise failed to meet the RTO: convene a lessons-learned meeting and update the DR plan.
4. Annually: present the continuity readiness report to the board. Include: number of exercises conducted, RTO performance, BIA review status, and any open improvement actions.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **`last_exercise_date` is empty for new plans.** Plans created recently may never have been exercised. Fix: the SPL should handle null exercise dates: `eval exercise_overdue=if(isnull(last_exercise_date), 1, ...)`. A plan with no exercise history is, by definition, untested.

• **RTO measured in different units.** Some teams record RTO in hours, others in minutes. Fix: standardise to hours in the CSV. Add a validation note in the CSV header row.

• **Exercise scope doesn't match plan scope.** A DR exercise for "Cloud infrastructure" may not cover the full scope of the DR plan if only one cloud region was tested. Fix: add an `exercise_scope_coverage_pct` field (e.g., 75%) to record partial coverage. Surface plans with < 100% coverage.

• **ITSI service not mapped to BCP plan.** The ITSI service tree may use different naming than the BCP register's `scope` field. Fix: add an `itsi_service_id` column to the CSV and join directly to `itsi:kpi_summary`.

• **Multiple plans for the same scope.** Some organisations have overlapping plans (e.g., a corporate BCP and a site-specific DR plan for the same data centre). Fix: designate one as the primary plan for NIS2 evidence purposes. Add a `primary=yes/no` field and filter to `primary=yes` in the alert.
grandmaExplanation: We track two practical things: when we last practiced how to keep important systems running in a crisis, and whether real disruptions stayed inside the time we promised. That way, when oversight asks, we have plain dates and numbers—not frantic emails.
